tools.name: nika:assert
tools.description: Fail-fast guard: fail the task when condition is false (else no-op).

tools.name: nika:chart
tools.description: Render a DETERMINISTIC chart artifact from rows + a semantic spec (bar | line | area_band | scatter | heatmap) — byte-identical SVG saved at `out`, sha256 in outputs (the trace-chain receipt) · optional Vega-Lite sibling via compile_to. Pure compute + one permit-gated write: no network, no clock, re-runs are idempotent.

args.name: data
args.desc: rows · array of flat objects (strings + numbers) · or { path: <json file> }

args.name: semantics
args.desc: field → usd | duration_ms | tokens | count | delta | percent | timestamp | category (drives formatting + palettes · delta ⇒ diverging anchored 0)

args.name: chart
args.desc: { type, x, y, y_lo?, y_hi? (area_band bounds), y2? (actual overlay), color? (series split · heatmap value), title?, width?, height? } — x/y/… are field names

args.name: out
args.desc: artifact path ending .svg (fs-permit gated · parents created · idempotent)

args.name: compile_to
args.desc: vega_lite — also writes the .vl.json sibling next to the svg

tools.name: nika:compose
tools.description: Statically check a Nika workflow draft you wrote · returns the full `nika check` verdict as JSON (conformance + secret-flow + permits + the termination/cost certificate) · NEVER executes it. Iterate until valid, then deliver the draft. Agent loops only.

tools.name: nika:convert
tools.description: Convert between json·yaml·toml·csv (from/to · identity rejected).

args.name: formula_guard
args.desc: CSV only · prefix formula-triggering cells (=+-@) with ' against spreadsheet injection · default false (alters data)

tools.name: nika:date
tools.description: Timestamp arithmetic · op-discriminated (now|add|subtract|format|parse|diff) · strftime grammar · ISO 8601 out.

args.name: op
args.desc: now | add | subtract | format | parse | diff

args.name: input
args.desc: the timestamp to render (format) or the text to read (parse)

args.name: unit
args.desc: seconds (default) | milliseconds | minutes | hours | days | weeks (diff)

tools.name: nika:done
tools.description: Mark the current agent loop complete (the loop-completion sentinel · agent loops only).

tools.name: nika:edit
tools.description: In-place literal find/replace (all occurrences · count caps it). NOT regex.

tools.name: nika:emit
tools.description: Emit a custom machine event for subscribers/journal (distinct from log).

tools.name: nika:fetch
tools.description: HTTP request + content extraction · returns the extracted body (non-2xx is an error). SSRF-defended.

args.name: method
args.desc: GET (default) | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH | HEAD

args.name: headers
args.desc: string map · auth rides here: { x-api-key: "${{ secrets.KEY }}" }

args.name: body
args.desc: request body (objects auto-JSON) · at most one of body/form/multipart

args.name: multipart
args.desc: multipart/form-data parts · {name, value} text or {name, path, filename?, content_type?} file (path is permits.fs.read-gated · ≤32 MiB total) · POST/PUT/PATCH only

args.name: traverse
args.desc: bounded same-origin crawl · { max_pages: 1..=25 (required), respect_robots?: bool (default true) } · GET only · excludes mode/selector/jq/body/form/multipart · emits { url, page_count, pages[], assets } page digests

args.name: mode
args.desc: markdown (default) | article | text | selector | jq | metadata | links | feed | sitemap | raw

args.name: jq
args.desc: a jq expression (mode: jq only · the one data language)

tools.name: nika:glob
tools.description: Glob match · returns paths sorted lexicographically.

args.name: exclude
args.desc: a glob pattern or a list of them to drop from the result

tools.name: nika:grep
tools.description: Recursive regex search · returns {path,line,match} sorted by (path,line).

tools.name: nika:hash
tools.description: Content hashing · blake3 (default) | sha256 | sha512 · hex (default) or base64.

tools.name: nika:image_fx
tools.description: Apply deterministic artistic effects to a PNG (dither · palette · duotone · pixelate · halftone · grain · vignette · chromatic_aberration · scanlines · glitch · ascii) — byte-identical forever for identical input+args; the recipe rides the artifact (tEXt) and outputs carry path + sha256, bytes never inline.

args.name: input
args.desc: source PNG path (read · permit-gated fs.read · PNG v1: produce upstream via image_generate format png)

args.name: out
args.desc: output artifact path (.png · .txt/.ans for ascii text/ansi emits) — rides the declared permits.fs boundary

args.name: ops
args.desc: ordered single-key op maps · resize{width,height,filter} · crop{x,y,width,height} · levels{brightness,contrast} · grayscale · palette_map{palette} · dither{mode,palette} · duotone{dark,light} · pixelate{block} · halftone{cell,angle} · grain{intensity} · vignette{strength} · chromatic_aberration{shift} · scanlines{strength,period} · glitch{line_shift,channel_shift,blocks} · ascii{cols,emit} (ascii must be last)

args.name: seed
args.desc: stochastic-op seed (grain · glitch) · default 0 · the seed IS the style

tools.name: nika:image_generate
tools.description: Generate image assets (local compat servers · openai gpt-image-2 · gemini gemini-3.1-flash-image · xai grok-imagine-image · mock for offline runs) — saves files under output_dir and returns paths + dimensions + sha256 (+ a provenance manifest); image bytes never ride outputs.

args.name: provider
args.desc: local | openai | gemini | xai | mock (inferred from model: when omitted · local is never inferred)

args.name: model
args.desc: provider model id · defaults: stablediffusion (local · LocalAI convention) · gpt-image-2 · gemini-3.1-flash-image · grok-imagine-image · mock-image-1

args.name: prompt
args.desc: the creative brief the provider renders

args.name: image
args.desc: mode:edit · one source image PATH (read · permit-gated fs.read)

args.name: images
args.desc: mode:edit · source image PATHS (multi-ref · capped per provider)

args.name: mask
args.desc: mode:edit · optional pixel-mask PATH (openai/local only · refused elsewhere)

args.name: n
args.desc: variant count 1..=10 (gemini runs n sequential calls)

args.name: size
args.desc: exact WIDTHxHEIGHT (gpt-image-2 arbitrary /16 sizes · gemini folds to a size class) or auto — an exact size wins over aspect_ratio

args.name: quality
args.desc: auto (default) | low | medium | high | ultra (folds to high on openai · model-tier on gemini)

args.name: format
args.desc: png (default) | jpeg | webp — magic bytes are the authority on what lands

args.name: background
args.desc: auto (default) | transparent (needs png/webp and a supporting model) | opaque

args.name: seed
args.desc: gemini best-effort · openai has no seed (warned + dropped)

args.name: reference_images
args.desc: media roadmap — rejected loudly in V1

args.name: provider_options
args.desc: vetted pass-through · openai {moderation, user} · gemini {thinking_level, image_size} · xai {user, resolution}

args.name: output_dir
args.desc: directory the assets land in (rides the declared permits.fs boundary)

args.name: filename_prefix
args.desc: filename stem (else metadata.page_slug, else `image`) — sanitized, traversal-free

args.name: save
args.desc: V1 contract: stays true — assets land on disk, never inline

args.name: manifest
args.desc: write the provenance manifest JSON beside the assets (default true)

args.name: metadata
args.desc: free provenance fields (campaign · page_slug · locale …) echoed to output + manifest

args.name: debug
args.desc: echo the sanitized raw provider response (base64 stripped)

tools.name: nika:inspect
tools.description: Introspect the running workflow · view: cost | records | dag_info | threads.

tools.name: nika:jq
tools.description: Run a jq expression over input · THE data-transform language (map·filter·select·reshape). Emits exactly one value.

args.name: input
args.desc: any JSON value (or an array for multi-input ops)

tools.name: nika:json_diff
tools.description: JSON diff · returns an RFC 6902 JSON Patch.

tools.name: nika:json_merge_patch
tools.description: RFC 7396 merge patch (null deletes a key) over a target object.

tools.name: nika:log
tools.description: Emit a human-readable diagnostic log line to the run stream (best-effort · never fails).

tools.name: nika:notify
tools.description: Send a notification · v0.1 engines support the webhook channel.

args.name: data
args.desc: optional structured context (any JSON · rides the payload beside the message)

tools.name: nika:prompt
tools.description: Ask a human (confirm|input|choice). Blocks until answered; uses default when headless.

args.name: default
args.desc: fallback when no human can answer

tools.name: nika:read
tools.description: Read a file · returns its text (or opaque bytes with binary: true).

tools.name: nika:tts_generate
tools.description: Synthesize speech audio (local compat servers · openai gpt-4o-mini-tts · elevenlabs · mock for offline runs) — saves ONE audio file under output_dir and returns path + format + sha256 + duration (+ a provenance manifest); audio bytes never ride outputs.

args.name: provider
args.desc: local | openai | elevenlabs | mock (inferred from model: when omitted · local is never inferred)

args.name: model
args.desc: provider model id · defaults: tts-1 (local · LocalAI convention) · gpt-4o-mini-tts · eleven_multilingual_v2 · mock-tts-1

args.name: text
args.desc: the text to speak · ≤4096 chars (fan long scripts out with for_each)

args.name: voice
args.desc: provider voice · defaults: alloy (openai/local) · Rachel's id (elevenlabs) · sine (mock)

args.name: format
args.desc: mp3 | wav | auto (default · provider-native · the saved extension follows the bytes)

args.name: output_dir
args.desc: directory the audio lands in (created if missing · fs-permit gated)

args.name: manifest
args.desc: write the sidecar provenance manifest (default true)

args.name: metadata
args.desc: free-form provenance echoed into output + manifest

tools.name: nika:uuid
tools.description: Generate a UUID · v7 (default · sortable) or v4 (random).

tools.name: nika:validate
tools.description: Validate data against a JSON Schema · returns {valid, errors} (invalid data is a report, not a failure).

args.name: data
args.desc: the value (or a YAML string with format: yaml)

tools.name: nika:wait
tools.description: Pause: relative duration (e.g. "30s") XOR absolute until (ISO 8601). Exactly one.

tools.name: nika:write
tools.description: Write a file · returns the path. overwrite defaults true · create_dirs defaults false.
